tool.name: recetox-xMSannotator: Simple Annotation
tool.description: perform simple annotation by matching peaks to compound database

param.name: metadata_table
param.label: Peak metadata table
param.help: Peak metadata table from recetox-aplcms or similar tool. Required columns: id, mz, rt. Additional columns are ignored.

param.name: compound_table
param.label: Compound database
param.help: Database of compounds for annotation. Required columns: compound_id, monoisotopic_mass, molecular_formula.

param.name: adduct_table
param.label: Adduct database (optional)
param.help: Database of adducts. If provided, compound-adduct pairs will be generated. Required columns: adduct, charge, mass, n_molecules.

tool.help:
**Simple Annotation**
Perform initial annotation by matching measured peaks to a compound database based on mass tolerance.
Description ===========
This tool performs the first step of the xMSannotator pipeline: simple annotation. It generates all possible compound-adduct pairs (if an adduct table is provided) and matches these pairs to measured peaks based on mass tolerance.
A compound-adduct pair is considered a match to a peak when the mass difference is within the specified tolerance (in ppm).
Inputs ------
**Peak Metadata Table** A table containing peak information with at least three columns: - `id`: Unique identifier for each peak - `mz`: Mass-to-charge ratio - `rt`: Retention time
**Compound Database** A database of known compounds with: - `compound_id`: Unique compound identifier - `monoisotopic_mass`: Exact mass of the compound - `molecular_formula`: Chemical formula
**Adduct Database (Optional)** If provided, defines common adduct forms: - `adduct`: Adduct name (e.g., "[M+H]+", "[M-H]-") - `charge`: Charge of the adduct - `mass`: Mass contribution of the adduct - `n_molecules`: Number of molecules in the adduct
**Mass Tolerance [ppm]** Maximum allowed mass difference in parts per million for matching.
Outputs -------
**Annotation Table** A table containing initial annotations with columns including: - `peak`: Peak identifier - `mz`: Measured m/z - `rt`: Retention time - `compound_id`: Matched compound identifier - `adduct`: Adduct form (if applicable) - `mz_error`: Mass error in ppm
Example Use Case ----------------
This tool is typically used as the first step in a multi-step annotation workflow:
1. **Simple Annotation** → Generate initial peak-compound matches 2. **Compute Mass Defect** → Add mass defect column 3. **Compute Correlations** → Analyze peak correlations 4. **Compute Modules** → Group correlated peaks 5. **Further analysis...**
References ----------
Novotný, J., Čech, M., & Troják, M. (2024). recetox-xMSannotator: Advanced metabolite annotation tool. Analytical Chemistry, 96(XX), XXXX-XXXX. DOI: 10.1021/acs.analchem.6b01214
